question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a misconfigured `shard` value in `CommonProperties` (e.g., shard=1 instead of shard=0) to cause `SystemEntity.toEntityId()` to map `treasuryAccount()` (entity num 2) to the wrong shard, causing the mirror node to misidentify treasury transactions in Hashgraph history and corrupt financial record integrity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `networkPropertyFile()` (entity 121) and `hapiPermissionFile()` (entity 122) are resolved using attacker-influenced `shard`/`realm` values, causing the mirror node to read network property and permission updates from wrong entity IDs and accept tampered governance changes as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a `realm` value injection into `CommonProperties` to cause `SystemEntity.toEntityId()` to map `throttleDefinitionFile()` (entity 123) to a wrong entity ID, causing the mirror node to apply incorrect throttle definitions from Hashgraph history and either allow unbounded transaction throughput or block legitimate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `exchangeRateFile()` (entity 112) entity ID resolution via `toEntityId()` with a manipulated `shard`/`realm` to cause the mirror node to read exchange rate data from a wrong entity in Hashgraph history, corrupting all fee calculations derived from that exchange rate?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `feeScheduleFile()` (entity 111) and `simpleFeeScheduleFile()` (entity 113) entity ID resolution to cause the mirror node to read fee schedule data from wrong entities in Hashgraph history, enabling the attacker to make the mirror node report incorrect fee schedules to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@Lazy` annotation on `dataSource()` to delay DataSource initialization until first use, creating a window between application startup and first database access during which a tampered database can be substituted, causing the mirror node to ingest fabricated Hashgraph history on first connection?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the credential resolution order in `dataSource()` (lines 75-84) where `JdbcConnectionDetails` overrides `DataSourceProperties`, by registering a crafted `JdbcConnectionDetails` bean that provides credentials to an attacker-controlled database containing fabricated Hashgraph history records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged user inject a `spring.datasource.hikari.dataSourceProperties` entry that sets a PostgreSQL session-level parameter (e.g., `search_path`) via the `HikariConfig` bean, causing all Hashgraph history queries to resolve table names against an attacker-controlled schema containing tampered data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged user set `spring.datasource.hikari.minimumIdle=0` and `spring.datasource.hikari.idleTimeout=1` to cause the connection pool to constantly cycle connections, and exploit the gap between connection teardown and re-establishment to inject tampered Hashgraph history records during the reconnection window?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase()` exception swallowing (lines 51-57) where connection errors are only logged and not propagated, to mask a successful connection to a tampered database replica that passes `connection.isValid()` but contains fabricated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `connection.isValid(Math.toIntExact(properties.getValidationTimeout().toSeconds()))` check (line 44-45) in `DatabaseWaiter` by providing a database that passes `isValid()` but has been pre-loaded with tampered Hashgraph history, since `isValid()` only checks connectivity and not data integrity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user set `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.enabled=false` via an externally reachable configuration path to disable the `DatabaseWaiter` check entirely, causing the mirror node to connect to a database without any startup validation and immediately begin ingesting Hashgraph history from a potentially tampered source?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.connectTimeout` minimum of 1 second to cause the `DatabaseWaiter` to use an extremely short connect timeout, causing legitimate database connections to time out while an attacker's faster-responding tampered database replica is accepted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user craft API requests that cause `SpelHelper.hashCode(byte[])` to be called with byte arrays representing Hashgraph record file hashes, and exploit `Arrays.hashCode()` collision properties to cause two different record file hashes to map to the same cache key, serving a tampered record in place of a legitimate one?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user exploit `SpelHelper.hashCode(Object[])` returning `Arrays.hashCode()` (a 32-bit non-cryptographic hash) as a cache key component for Hashgraph history lookups, by crafting two different consensus record objects with the same `Arrays.hashCode()` value to cause cache poisoning with tampered history data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `CustomJsonFormatMapper` (set via `AvailableSettings.JSON_FORMAT_MAPPER`) to submit a crafted JSON string to a JSONB Hashgraph history column that, when round-tripped through `fromString()`/`toString()`, silently loses or mutates fields (e.g., consensus timestamp, running hash) due to `OBJECT_MAPPER` configuration gaps?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the shared `OBJECT_MAPPER` instance in `CustomJsonFormatMapper` (referenced via `ObjectToStringSerializer.OBJECT_MAPPER`) by triggering concurrent deserialization of Hashgraph history JSON payloads that cause thread-safety issues in the mapper, resulting in corrupted or cross-contaminated history records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@EnableConfigurationProperties(CommonProperties.class)` annotation to register a second `CommonProperties` bean with attacker-controlled `shard`/`realm` values, causing `SystemEntity` to be constructed with wrong parameters and misidentify all system entities in Hashgraph history processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@ConfigurationPropertiesScan('org.hiero.mirror')` scan to inject a competing `@ConfigurationProperties('hiero.mirror.common')` bean that overrides `CommonProperties`, setting `realm` to a large value that causes `SystemEntity.toEntityId()` to generate entity IDs that never match any real Hashgraph history records, effectively blinding the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `new HikariDataSource(config)` construction (line 94) with a crafted `jdbcUrl` containing JDBC URL parameters (e.g., `?currentSchema=attacker`) that redirect all Hashgraph history table reads to an attacker-controlled schema within the same database instance?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `dataSourceProperties.determinePassword()` call (line 77) to inject a password containing special characters that, when passed to `DriverManager.getConnection()`, are interpreted as JDBC URL parameters and redirect the connection to a tampered Hashgraph history database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `jdbcProperties.setProperty('password', password)` call (line 32-34) in `DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase()` where `password != null` check allows an empty string password, causing the waiter to connect to a database with no authentication and accept a tampered Hashgraph history database as valid?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `StringUtils.hasText(username)` check (line 28-30) in `DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase()` to bypass username validation by providing a whitespace-only username that passes `hasText()` as false, causing the waiter to connect without a username and accept an unauthenticated tampered database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `AvailableSettings.GENERATE_STATISTICS=true` setting to cause Hibernate to log detailed query statistics including Hashgraph history table names, column names, and query patterns, enabling the attacker to craft targeted SQL injection or data manipulation payloads against specific history tables?
